Tests the behavior of Math.max when given an object, expecting a result of NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the `Math.max` function when it is given an object as an argument. The `isNaN` function checks if the result of `Math.max({})` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is the expected outcome when `Math.max` receives a non-numeric input. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific test case to ensure that `Math.max` handles non-numeric inputs correctly.
